title: On The Burning Of Lord Mansfield�s Library, Together With His Mss., By The Mob, In The Month Of June 1780.
content:
So then�the Vandals of our isle,
    Sworn foes to sense and law,
    Have burnt to dust a nobler pile
    Than ever Roman saw!


    And Murray sighs o�er Pope and Swift,
    And many a treasure more,
    The well-judged purchase and the gift
    That graced his letter�d store.


    Their pages mangled, burnt, and torn,
    The loss was his alone;
    But ages yet to come shall mourn
    The burning of his own.
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On The Burning Of Lord Mansfield�s Library, Together With His Mss., By The Mob, In The Month Of June 1780."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
